purpose: AI-readable catalog for choosing optional Godot gameplay modules. Modules are not enabled by default in the empty starter template.
rules: Prefer PrototypeState.gd, scenes/Game.gd, and Hud.gd for first-version gameplay work.
Use modules only when the ability has a clear single responsibility or repeated use.
Modules communicate through signals and GameEvents; they must not directly edit HUD or PrototypeState internals.
Data modules should expose serialize() and deserialize(data) when persistence is needed.

modules.name: InputProfile
modules.responsibility: Register and read default movement/action inputs.
modules.when_to_use: A game needs WASD/arrow keys, primary action, secondary action, or cancel actions.

modules.name: PlayerController2D
modules.when_to_use: A 2D action/survival game needs reusable direct movement.

modules.name: EnemyAI2D
modules.responsibility: Lightweight chase/wander AI without damage or win/loss logic.
modules.when_to_use: The first version needs simple moving threats.

modules.name: Spawner
modules.responsibility: Instantiate PackedScene nodes and emit spawn events.
modules.when_to_use: Enemies, pickups, obstacles, or props need centralized spawning.

modules.name: Pickup
modules.responsibility: Area2D pickup entry that emits collected payloads.
modules.when_to_use: A game has collectible items, score gems, resources, or keys.

modules.name: ItemCatalog
modules.responsibility: Store item definitions, stack limits, item types, and equip slots.
modules.when_to_use: Inventory, loot, equipment, or quest items need stable definitions.

modules.name: InventoryStore
modules.responsibility: Slot inventory with stacking, removal, moving, and persistence.
modules.when_to_use: A game needs carried items or backpack pressure.

modules.name: EquipmentStore
modules.when_to_use: Items can be equipped and affect stats.

modules.name: LootTable
modules.responsibility: Weighted loot roll returning item ids and quantities.
modules.when_to_use: Enemies, chests, rewards, or extraction loot need random drops.

modules.name: WaveDirector
modules.responsibility: Timed wave sequence that emits spawn requests.
modules.when_to_use: Survival, tower defense, arena, or stage games need wave pacing.

modules.name: QuestStore
modules.responsibility: Quest definitions, objective progress, completion, and persistence.
modules.when_to_use: A game needs task goals, chapters, or objective tracking beyond a single score.

modules.name: DialogueRunner
modules.responsibility: Advance dialogue lines without binding to a UI skin.
modules.when_to_use: A game needs tutorial, story, NPC, or mission text sequences.

modules.name: SaveStore
modules.responsibility: Register save providers and write/read JSON save data.

modules.name: MenuPresenter
modules.responsibility: Programmatic start/pause/result menu that emits menu actions.
modules.when_to_use: A game needs simple menus without custom UI art yet.

modules.name: SceneRouter
modules.responsibility: Central scene changes and reload requests.
modules.when_to_use: A game has multiple scenes or needs reliable reload/change failure reporting.

modules.name: TutorialHints
modules.responsibility: One-shot or repeatable tutorial hint triggers.
modules.when_to_use: A game needs contextual prompts for first-time actions.
